chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI C. P. No. D–2459 of 2025 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S). Priority 1. For hearing of CMA No.11657/2025. 2. for hearing of main case..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-. Dated 12.06.2025 Ms. Sofia Saeed Shah, Advocate for the petitioner. Mr. Jawad Dero AG Sindh alongwith Mr. Ali Safdar Deepar, AAG Sindh..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-. We have heard learned counsel for the petitioner as well as learned Advocate General Sindh. It appears that the relief sought is almost the same as was given vide order dated 03.03.2025 in C.P. No.D-850 of 2025. Learned A.G. Sindh has no objection to similar relief being granted to the petitioner. Accordingly based on the same, this petition is disposed of alongwith listed application in terms of the said order which is reproduced below for ease of reference: “1. Granted. 2-4. Learned AAG is present and waives notice. It is jointly submitted that the present petition pertains to the vires of imposition of Infrastructure Cess. It is added that the matter has already been determined by a Division Bench of this Court in the case of Ibrahim Fibres Ltd. vs. Province of Sindh and others (C.P.D 3309 of 2011 judgment dated 04.06.2021). Learned counsel for the petitioner submits that the ratio of the aforesaid judgment is squarely applicable to the present facts and circumstances, hence, this petition may be dismissed in mutatis mutandis application thereof, leaving the petitioner at liberty to seek appellate remedy along with other similarly placed parties. Learned AAG submits that he has no objection to the dismissal of the petition in this manner as aforesaid.

Learned counsel for the petitioner submits that the ratio of the aforesaid judgment is squarely applicable to the present facts and circumstances, hence, this petition may be dismissed in mutatis mutandis application thereof, leaving the petitioner at liberty to seek appellate remedy along with other similarly placed parties. Learned AAG submits that he has no objection to the dismissal of the petition in this manner as aforesaid. Order accordingly. The instant petition is dismissed.” HEAD OF CONST. BENCHES JUDGE Asif
